title: Pleione pleionoides
taxonomy: Pleione pleionoides is classified as a species within the genus Pleione, which is a group of orchid plants. The genus Pleione is part of the subfamily Epidendroideae, which is a subfamily of orchids. This subfamily is further classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. The family Orchidaceae is a diverse group of flowering plants that consists of approximately 30,000 species. The classification of Pleione pleionoides reflects its evolutionary relationships with other orchid species.

intro: Pleione pleionoides, commonly known as the Himalayan Pleione, is a species of orchid native to the Himalayan region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, contributing to its potential health benefits such as wound healing, pain relief, and protection against infections. The bioactive constituents of Pleione pleionoides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems.
